mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of observed cases during slowdown periods, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in regime-filtered tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index falls below 2.0% year-over-year, disinflationary pressures allow central banks to maintain accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth remains below 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals healthy capacity utilization and supply chain efficiency, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of expansion-phase observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance levels.
